Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: force-fill hidden security answer input on FADV secretQuestion page

GWT renders input[name="answer"] as display:none behind a styled widget.
Playwright's waitForSelector with state:"visible" waited 30s for it to
become visible (resolved 65x as hidden) before timing out.

Fix: use state:"attached" (element is in DOM — confirmed by Path A scan)
and pass force:true to .fill() and .click() to bypass Playwright's
visibility guard, writing directly to the hidden native input.

commit.message:
Fjern COEP, COOP og form-action fra security headers

COEP (Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy) og COOP
(Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy) bryter NextAuth sin OAuth-flyt og
cookie/CSRF-håndtering. Fjernet helt - de er kun nødvendige for
SharedArrayBuffer som ikke er kritisk. Fjernet også form-action
CSP-direktivet som blokkerte OAuth-redirects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(packer): use SetupComplete.cmd for WinRM on cloned VMs

Replace broken FirstLogonCommands/AutoLogon pattern with
SetupComplete.cmd — the Microsoft-recommended mechanism for
post-sysprep bootstrapping (used by MDT, SCCM, enterprise builds).

FirstLogonCommands require AutoLogon to fire, but cleanup.ps1
strips AutoLogon credentials before sysprep. After clone, no one
logs in, so WinRM never gets re-enabled.

SetupComplete.cmd runs as LOCAL SYSTEM before the logon screen
with no AutoLogon dependency. It enables WinRM, sets network
profile to Private, and self-deletes after first run.

Also adds hostname rename to domain join playbook so Ansible sets
the computer name atomically during domain join.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: add date range computation instructions to CODE_EXEC_SYSTEM prompt

Many analytics tasks involve time-based filtering: 'last 6 quarters',
'past 3 months', 'previous 5 weeks'. Without instructions, the model
guesses at date calculation — often wrongly.

Added explicit guidance:
- Parse 'Today's date: YYYY-MM-DD' from context (it's there for these tasks)
- Quarter = 3 months, week = 7 days
- Use datetime.strptime for ISO dates
- Safe fallback if dateutil unavailable

commit.message:
Fix Vercel build error: Remove circular dependency in Tailwind CSS

Fixed build failure caused by circular dependency in @layer utilities.
The issue was using @apply font-display in the same layer where
.font-display was defined, causing Tailwind to fail during build.

Changes:
- Replaced @apply font-display with direct font-family property
- Changed from: @apply font-display text-2xl...
- Changed to: font-family: 'Georgia', ...; @apply text-2xl...

This resolves the Vercel deployment error:
"Cannot apply unknown utility class 'font-display'"

Build now completes successfully both locally and on Vercel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Introduce a Skin interface that decouples the manager panel rendering
and navigation from the core application model. This enables
experimenting with different UI layouts while sharing the same tmux
pane management, data layer, and action keys.

commit.message:
refactor: replace Hz calibration with total_ticks/speed for orbital sync

The old approach estimated server Hz empirically from poll deltas, then
used ticks/hz to compute game_seconds. Hz measurement noise × large
total_ticks caused snaps exceeding the drift threshold every poll.

The correct conversion is simply game_seconds = total_ticks / speed:
server fires ticks at speed/TICK_INTERVAL per real-sec, each adding
TICK_INTERVAL*speed, so total_ticks/real_sec = speed^2, and
game_seconds/real_sec = speed, giving game_seconds = total_ticks/speed.

New logic: anchor once at login, run locally like single-player after
that, re-sync only if drift >86400 game-sec (genuine disconnect).
Speed changes re-anchor on next poll via scale_server_rate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Apply alt glyphs selectively to A/E/T/F only, not all characters

font-feature-settings: "aalt" 1 was applying alternates to every character
with alternates (M, N, R, H, K, V, W, Y too), causing wrong glyphs.
Now wraps only A, E, T, F in per-character spans with "aalt" 1 so other
characters render normally.

- altGlyphs.ts: returns ReactNode with A/E/T/F wrapped in aalt spans
- H1/H2/Tagline: remove global fontFeatureSettings (handled by spans)
- [slug].js: add wrapAltGlyphs() for HTML span wrapping, use innerHTML
- [slug].js links page: remove aalt from SVG (no per-char control there)

fix(perf): Disable AI review step to prevent 502 timeout on Railway

The review_worksheet() second Gemini call adds ~3-5s latency on top of
the main generation call, pushing total time past Railway's timeout.
Disabled for now — the quality gate + light_validator already catch the
same issues. Can re-enable as async background job later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite tuner pitch detection for accuracy and stability

- New normalized autocorrelation algorithm (guitar range only: 70-380 Hz)
- FIRST-peak detection instead of highest-peak: eliminates octave errors
  where harmonics score higher than the fundamental
- 24-sample median ring buffer + outlier rejection (>80 cents from median)
  replaces simple EMA — robust against spurious readings
- Throttled to 30fps analysis (every 2nd frame) for performance
- smoothingTimeConstant=0 on analyser (we smooth ourselves)
- Parabolic interpolation for sub-sample frequency precision

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(core): clean up RecursiveModuleLoad API (#32424)

## Summary

Cleans up the `RecursiveModuleLoad` API in `deno_core` by addressing
several long-standing design issues:

- **Remove `RefCell` hack**: `LoadInit::Side.code` was wrapped in
`RefCell<Option<String>>` solely because `prepare(&self)` needed to
`.take()` it. Changing `prepare` to `&mut self` removes the need for
interior mutability.
- **Eliminate triple resolution**: The root specifier was resolved
independently in `new()`, `prepare()`, and `poll_next(Init)`. Now it's
resolved once in `new()` and cached in a `resolved_specifier` field,
reused by the other two call sites.
- **Merge construct + prepare into async constructors**: `main()` and
`side()` are now `async` and call `prepare()` internally, removing the
need for the `ModuleMap::load_main` / `ModuleMap::load_side` wrapper
functions.
- **Privatize leaked fields**: `id`, `root_module_id`, and
`root_module_reference` were `pub` fields set as side effects. They're
now private with `pub(crate)` getter methods.
- **Add `run_to_completion()` method**: Encapsulates the identical
while-let-next pump loop that was copy-pasted across 3 call sites
(`load_main_es_module_from_code`, `load_side_es_module_from_code`,
`do_load_job`). The dynamic import path (`drain_dyn_imports`) remains
poll-based and continues using the `Stream` directly.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve TRELLIS.2 success rate: blender default, early-out, vertex colors

Switch render backend to Blender by default for higher-quality conditioning
images closer to TRELLIS.2 training distribution. Add early-out after 3
consecutive empty voxel results to avoid wasting GPU time on hopeless models.
Preserve original vertex/face colors in trimesh fallback instead of always
flattening to gray. Reduce MAX_MODELS_PER_RUN (250->150) and increase
RESTART_SLEEP_SEC (8->15) to mitigate heap fragmentation.
